name: Mamtu
description: Steamed dumplings stuffed with spiced minced meat and onions, served with a tangy tomato-yogurt sauce. A Baltistan classic.

name: Balay
description: A hearty Balti soup made with hand-pulled wheat dumplings, meat and local herbs. Warming and filling, perfect for cold evenings.

name: Prapu
description: Soft buckwheat pancakes, a staple bread of Baltistan, usually eaten with butter or apricot oil.

name: Khambir
description: Traditional leavened local bread baked in a tandoor, slightly dense and nutty in flavour.

name: Gur Gur Chai
description: Salted butter tea, churned the traditional way in a wooden churner. Best paired with Khambir.

name: Chapshoro
description: A round stuffed bread filled with minced meat, onion and local spices, baked until golden.

name: Skardu Daal
description: Local lentils slow-cooked with mountain herbs and a light tempering, served with Khambir or rice.

name: Dried Apricots (Khost)
description: Sun-dried apricots from Baltistan orchards, naturally sweet and chewy. No added sugar.

name: Apricot Kernels
description: Sweet apricot seed kernels, a popular snack in Gilgit-Baltistan, similar in taste to almonds.

name: Walnuts (in shell)
description: Thin-shelled local walnuts, easy to crack, rich and slightly sweet kernel inside.

name: Dried Mulberries
description: Naturally sun-dried mulberries, a traditional energy snack used in winters.

name: Apricot Oil
description: Cold-pressed apricot kernel oil, used for cooking and as a traditional skin care oil.
